campaign_name.title: Campaign Name
campaign_name.tooltip: Choose a name for your campaign. This will be used to identify saved games.
campaign_name.help: Your campaign name should be memorable and help you identify this playthrough among multiple saves.

campaign_type.title: Campaign Type
campaign_type.tooltip: Select the overall structure of your campaign experience.
campaign_type.help: Campaign type determines the pacing, difficulty scaling, and win conditions for your campaign.

options.standard: Follow the complete Five Parsecs experience with all phases and progression.
options.quick_start: Jump into action with simplified setup and faster progression.
options.story_driven: Focus on narrative elements with enhanced story events.
options.sandbox: Open-ended play without specific victory conditions.

victory_condition.title: Victory Condition
victory_condition.tooltip: Define how you win the campaign.
victory_condition.help: Victory conditions give your campaign direction and a satisfying conclusion when achieved.

story_track.title: Story Track
story_track.tooltip: Select a narrative arc to follow throughout your campaign.
story_track.help: Story tracks provide themed missions, unique encounters, and narrative progression.

options.none: No specific narrative - pure sandbox experience.
options.rags_to_riches: Start with nothing, build an empire.
options.revenge: Hunt down those who wronged you.
options.discovery: Uncover ancient secrets of the galaxy.

tutorial_mode.title: Tutorial Mode
tutorial_mode.help: Tutorial mode provides helpful hints and explanations as you play.

options.off: No tutorial assistance - for experienced players.

creation_method.title: Creation Method
creation_method.tooltip: Choose how to generate your captain.
creation_method.help: Different methods offer varying levels of customization and randomness.

options.random: Roll stats randomly using 2D6÷3 formula. Quick and unpredictable.
options.custom: Manually allocate points to stats. Full control over character build.
options.veteran: Start with higher stats and experience. For experienced players.
options.import: Import a character from another campaign or save.

combat.title: Combat
combat.tooltip: Offensive capability in ranged and melee combat.
combat.help: Higher Combat improves hit chance. Captains get +1 bonus.

reactions.title: Reactions
reactions.help: Determines action order and reaction shots. Captains get +1 bonus.

toughness.title: Toughness
toughness.help: Higher Toughness reduces severity of wounds taken.

savvy.title: Savvy
savvy.help: Used for hacking, repairs, and finding loot. Captains get +1 bonus.

tech.title: Tech
tech.help: Affects use of high-tech equipment and gadgets.

speed.title: Speed
speed.help: Standard is 4 inches. Modified by gear and species.

luck.title: Luck
luck.tooltip: Fortune points for rerolls and saves.
luck.help: Captains start with 2 Luck. Can be spent to avoid death or reroll dice.

background.title: Captain Background
background.help: Background determines starting stat bonuses, credits, and sometimes gear or contacts.

motivation.title: Captain Motivation
motivation.tooltip: What drives your captain to adventure.
motivation.help: Motivation provides additional bonuses and may affect story events.

verbose_mode.title: Verbose Mode
verbose_mode.tooltip: Show the actual dice rolls during character generation.
verbose_mode.help: Enable this to see exactly what was rolled for each stat, useful for verification or learning the system.

crew_size.title: Starting Crew Size
crew_size.tooltip: Number of crew members to start with.
crew_size.help: Standard is 4-6 crew. More crew = more firepower but higher upkeep.

crew_member.title: Crew Member
crew_member.help: Crew members have their own stats, backgrounds, and can advance through experience.

ship_type.title: Ship Type
ship_type.help: Ships determine travel speed, cargo capacity, and available upgrades.

ship_name.title: Ship Name
ship_name.help: Give your ship a name that reflects its personality or your crew's reputation.

starting_gear.title: Starting Equipment
starting_gear.tooltip: Weapons and gear for your crew.
starting_gear.help: Equipment is automatically generated based on backgrounds. You can trade or purchase more later.

credits.title: Starting Credits
credits.help: Credits are used for repairs, purchases, fuel, and crew upkeep.

world_name.title: Starting World
world_name.tooltip: Your initial location in the galaxy.
world_name.help: Your starting world determines available jobs, threats, and initial opportunities.

tech_level.title: Tech Level
tech_level.help: 1-2: Primitive, 3-4: Standard, 5-6: Advanced. Affects available equipment and prices.

danger_level.title: Danger Level
danger_level.tooltip: Overall threat level of the world.
danger_level.help: Higher danger means tougher enemies but better rewards.

government.title: Government Type
government.help: Government affects law enforcement, black market access, and patron availability.

opportunities.title: Opportunities
opportunities.help: Patrons offer missions. Trade opportunities let you buy/sell goods.

threats.title: Known Threats
threats.help: Rivals, environmental hazards, and criminal elements that may affect your missions.

review.title: Campaign Review
review.tooltip: Review all your choices before starting.
review.help: This is your last chance to go back and make changes before beginning your campaign.

turn_structure.title: Campaign Turn
turn_structure.help: Each turn consists of: Travel → World Phase → Battle → Post-Battle. Complete all phases to advance to the next turn.

travel.title: Travel Phase
travel.help: Move between worlds, pay fuel costs, handle ship events.

world.title: World Phase
world.help: Recruit, trade, find jobs, assign crew tasks, and prepare for battle.

battle.title: Battle Phase
battle.help: Resolve the mission with tactical combat.

post_battle.title: Post-Battle Phase
post_battle.help: Collect loot, treat injuries, gain experience, advance the story.

stat_roll.title: Stat Generation (2D6÷3)
stat_roll.help: Roll 2D6, divide by 3 (round up). Results range from 1-4.

combat_roll.title: Combat Roll
combat_roll.help: Roll 1D6 + Combat stat. Hit on 5+ (modified by range and cover).

injury_roll.title: Injury Roll
injury_roll.help: When hit, roll 1D6 on injury table. Toughness reduces severity.
